meta.name: Senkessar — Index of the Ethiopian Synaxarium
meta.description: Maps each day of the 13-month Ethiopian calendar to its appointed Senkessar entry IDs. Entries are keyed by Ethiopian month name and day number. Each day may carry multiple entries (primary saint, secondary saint, feast). The 'reading_id' field is the component/narrative ID that will be resolved by renderOffice() via the eth-synaxarium-reading component.
meta.calendar_system: Ethiopian (Ge'ez) — 12 months of 30 days + Pagume (intercalary month of 5 or 6 days)
meta.note: Entry IDs follow the pattern: senkessar-{month-slug}-{day}. Narrative text files will reside at data/synaxarium/ethiopian/{month-slug}/{day}.json.

months.thematic_summary: The first month of the new Ethiopian year. Season of golden light, the Enkutatash New Year celebration, and the royal triumph of the Cross at Maskal. Opens with the Crown of the Year and builds toward the Finding of the True Cross on Day 17.

key_feasts.feast: Enkutatash — The Ethiopian New Year
The Birthday of the Virgin Mary
Maskal — The Feast of the Finding of the True Cross
Departure and Translation of St. John the Apostle
Monthly Crucifixion
Triple Feast (Maskal/Qal/Timqat)
St. Gregory the Illuminator, Apostle of Armenia

months.thematic_summary: The second month — the month of the Covenant of Mercy. As the excitement of the New Year settles, the focus turns inward: the quiet of the cell, the steadfastness of the martyrs, and the sustained faithfulness of the interior life. The critical historical anchor is the Martyrs of Najran and King Kaleb of Aksum on Day 24.

key_feasts.feast: St. Gebre Mesqel (King), Servant of the Cross
Martyrs of Najran and St. Elesbaan (King Kaleb of Aksum)
Monthly Crucifixion
Triple Feast (Maskal/Qal/Timqat)
St. John Chrysostom, the Golden-Mouthed

months.thematic_summary: The third month — the Apostolic Vesting. The interior life formed in Tiqimt finds its weight and purpose in community and mission. Four thematic anchors: the Four Heavenly Creatures (Day 8), the Great Feast of Archangel Michael (Day 12), the Entry of the Virgin into the Temple (Day 21), and the Twenty-Four Elders of Heaven (Day 24).

key_feasts.feast: The Four Heavenly Creatures (Arba'etu Insesa) — Support of the Throne
The Great Feast of Archangel Michael — Captain of the Host
Departure of St. Philip the Apostle — Ethiopia's Apostolic Patron
The Entry of the Virgin Mary into the Temple
The Twenty-Four Elders of Heaven — Heavenly Liturgy
Monthly Crucifixion
Triple Feast (Maskal/Qal/Timqat) + Dedication of the Church of the Nativity
Departure of St. Andrew the Apostle — First-Called

months.thematic_summary: The month of the Incarnation. The tone transitions from prophetic anticipation (Days 1–24) to the cosmic explosion of light at the Nativity (Day 25). The month moves from the groaning of creation to the cry of a newborn Child.

key_feasts.feast: The Eve of the Nativity
Ganna — The Nativity of Our Lord Jesus Christ
The Massacre of the Holy Innocents
Triple Feast (Monthly)
St. John Chrysostom (Monthly)

months.thematic_summary: The month of the Manifestation. The tone shifts from the interior mystery of the Nativity to the public, cosmic manifestation of the Trinity at the Jordan River. Timqat (Day 11) is the month's apex — the Hallowing of the Waters and the voice of the Father.

months.thematic_summary: The intercalary month — the five threshold days between the old year and the New Year (Meskerem 1). Governed by the theology of purification, the washing of the year's griefs in the rains of Pagumen, and the passage from the Old Man to the New. The primary feast is the Archangel Raphael on Day 3.

key_feasts.feast: The Great Feast of the Archangel Raphael, Healer and Guardian of the Waters

2 Pagumen — St. Titus the Apostle & The Assembly
3 Pagumen — The Archangel Raphael & The Assembly
4 Pagumen — St. Benjamin the Hermit & The Assembly
5 Pagumen — The Prophet Amos & The Assembly

entry_file_schema.description: Each reading_id resolves to a file at data/synaxarium/ethiopian/{month-slug}/{day}.json. That file must conform to the following schema.

schema.id: string — matches reading_id from this index (e.g. senkessar-meskerem-1)
schema.feast_title: string or null — name of the principal feast or commemoration

saints.name: string
saints.epithet: string or null — e.g. 'the Apostle', 'of Alexandria'
saints.narrative: string — the full Senkessar hagiographical reading for this saint

schema.closing_doxology: string or null — optional doxological formula appended after the reading
